schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: IBBI Registered Valuer — Securities & Financial Assets
schemas.description: IBBI Registered Valuer for Securities & Financial Assets. Registration IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333. Mandatory for Companies Act, IBC valuations. 100+ reports. CA V. Viswanathan, FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV.

step.text: Engagement letter with scope and purpose
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Issue signed report with IBBI registration number
step.name: Step 6

acceptedAnswer.text: A professional registered with IBBI under the Companies (Registered Valuers and Valuation) Rules, 2017. Mandatory for valuations under Companies Act Section 247 and IBC Regulation 35. CA V. Viswanathan holds IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333 for Securities & Financial Assets.

mainEntity.name: Is IBBI registration needed for FEMA valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not specifically. FEMA accepts valuation from a CA in practice or SEBI Merchant Banker. However, if the same transaction also triggers Companies Act requirements, a Registered Valuer is needed. Using a Registered Valuer for FEMA provides a higher standard of report.

Visit ibbi.gov.in → Information Utilities → Registered Valuers. Search by name (CA V. Viswanathan) or registration number (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333). The database shows asset class, RVO, and registration status.

mainEntity.name: What asset classes can an IBBI Registered Valuer cover?

acceptedAnswer.text: Three asset classes: (1) Land & Building, (2) Plant & Machinery, (3) Securities or Financial Assets. Each requires separate registration and examination. CA V. Viswanathan is registered for Securities or Financial Assets — covering all equity, debt, and hybrid instruments.

mainEntity.name: What asset classes does an IBBI Registered Valuer cover?

acceptedAnswer.text: Three classes: (1) Land and Building, (2) Plant and Machinery, (3) Securities and Financial Assets. CA V. Viswanathan is registered for Securities and Financial Assets — covering equity, debt, derivatives, and complex instruments.

mainEntity.name: How to verify an IBBI Registered Valuer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Search on IBBI website (ibbi.gov.in) under Registered Valuers section. Enter name or registration number. CA V. Viswanathan: IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333. Always verify before engaging any valuer.

mainEntity.name: Can a CA who is not an RV issue valuation reports?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, for FEMA (practicing CA is sufficient) and Income Tax Rule 11UA (CA or merchant banker). But for Companies Act Section 247 and IBC Regulation 35: IBBI Registered Valuer is mandatory. Using RV for all purposes provides higher standard.

mainEntity.name: What is the liability of a Registered Valuer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Valuer can be penalised by IBBI for: providing misleading reports, non-compliance with valuation standards, not maintaining independence. Penalty: warning, suspension, or cancellation of registration. Civil and criminal liability under Companies Act.

mainEntity.name: What continuing education does an RV need?

acceptedAnswer.text: 20 hours of structured CPE credits per year. Compliance monitored by Recognised Valuers Organisation (RVO). Non-compliance can lead to suspension. We exceed the minimum requirement.
